name: Google Sheets
description: Google Sheets API v4 — read, write, append, clear, and batch-mutate spreadsheets. Includes the generic spreadsheets:batchUpdate endpoint which exposes every formatting / sheet-management / find-replace operation Google supports.

tools.name: create_spreadsheet
tools.description: Create a new Google Sheets spreadsheet. Pass `properties.title` to set the name. Optionally pre-create sheets with `sheets[]`. Returns the full Spreadsheet object including spreadsheetId.

properties.description: Spreadsheet-level properties. Most useful: { title: string, locale: string, timeZone: string }.

sheets.description: Optional initial sheet definitions. Each entry is a Sheet object — minimum { properties: { title: 'Sheet1' } }.

tools.name: get_spreadsheet
tools.description: Get spreadsheet metadata: sheet names, sheet IDs, named ranges, properties. Use the `fields` mask to limit response size — for just sheet names, set `fields=sheets.properties`.

ranges.description: Optional A1 ranges to include grid data for. Without this only metadata is returned.

includeGridData.description: Include cell values + formatting in the response. Heavy — only set when needed.

fields.description: Comma-separated FieldMask to limit response size.

tools.name: get_by_filter
tools.description: Get spreadsheet by data filters — narrows the response to specific A1 ranges, GridRanges, or developer-metadata-tagged cells without including the entire grid. Use this when you know which cells you want but don't have a single contiguous A1 range.

dataFilters.description: Array of DataFilter objects. Each is one of: { a1Range: 'Sheet1!A1:B10' }, { gridRange: { sheetId, startRowIndex, endRowIndex, startColumnIndex, endColumnIndex } }, or { developerMetadataLookup: {...} }.

tools.name: batch_update
tools.description: Generic spreadsheet batchUpdate — the catch-all endpoint for every spreadsheet mutation Google supports. The agent passes a list of Request objects; each Request has exactly one operation key. Common operations: addSheet, deleteSheet, updateSheetProperties, updateSpreadsheetProperties, findReplace, insertDimension, deleteDimension, appendDimension, autoResizeDimensions, updateDimensionProperties, repeatCell (formatting), updateCells, mergeCells, unmergeCells, setBasicFilter, clearBasicFilter, addChart, addConditionalFormatRule, updateConditionalFormatRule, deleteConditionalFormatRule, setDataValidation, addProtectedRange, deleteProtectedRange, addNamedRange, deleteNamedRange, addBanding, deleteBanding, addFilterView, sortRange, cutPaste, copyPaste, randomizeRange, pasteData. Example for find/replace across all sheets: { spreadsheetId, requests: [{ findReplace: { find: 'old', replacement: 'new', allSheets: true, matchCase: false } }] }. See https://developers.google.com/sheets/api/reference/rest/v4/spreadsheets/request for the full Request schema.

requests.description: Array of Request objects. Each Request has exactly one operation key (e.g. addSheet, findReplace, repeatCell, …).

includeSpreadsheetInResponse.description: Return the updated spreadsheet in the response.

tools.name: read_range
tools.description: Read values from a single A1 range, e.g. 'Sheet1!A1:D10' or 'Sheet1!A:A' for a whole column. Returns a 2D array of cell values. For multiple ranges in one call use batch_get.

valueRenderOption.description: How values should be represented. FORMULA returns the literal cell formula instead of the computed value.

tools.name: write_range
tools.description: Write values to a single A1 range. Overwrites existing data in the range. Pass `values` as a 2D array of rows. Use valueInputOption='USER_ENTERED' (default) so '=SUM(A1:A10)' becomes a real formula and '12.5%' becomes a percentage; use 'RAW' to write strings literally with no parsing.

values.description: 2D array of rows. Each row is an array of cell values (strings, numbers, booleans, or formula strings starting with '=').

valueInputOption.description: How input is interpreted. USER_ENTERED parses formulas, dates, percentages, etc. like a human typing into the UI. RAW writes the exact string.

tools.name: append_rows
tools.description: Append rows after the last row containing data in a sheet. Unlike write_range, this finds the next empty row automatically. Use insertDataOption='INSERT_ROWS' (default) to push existing rows down, or 'OVERWRITE' to replace.

range.description: A1 notation of any cell in the table to append to. Google walks down the column to find the last data row.

tools.name: clear_range
tools.description: Clear values from a single A1 range. Keeps formatting and other cell properties — only the values are wiped.

tools.name: batch_get
tools.description: Read multiple A1 ranges in a single request. Pass `ranges` as an array of A1 strings. Returns one ValueRange per requested range.

tools.name: batch_get_by_filter
tools.description: Read multiple ranges selected by data filters (A1, GridRange, or developer metadata) in a single request. Use this when you want filter-style selection rather than a literal list of A1 ranges.

tools.name: batch_update_values
tools.description: Atomically write to multiple ranges in one request. Use when several updates need to land together. Pass `data` as an array of ValueRange objects ({ range, values, majorDimension? }).

data.description: Array of ValueRange objects. Example: [{ range: 'Sheet1!A1', values: [['hello']] }, { range: 'Sheet2!B2', values: [['world']] }].

valueInputOption.description: Required by the API. Note: lives in the BODY for batchUpdate (unlike single-range update where it's a query param).

tools.name: batch_update_values_by_filter
tools.description: Atomically write to multiple ranges selected by data filters. Pass `data` as an array of DataFilterValueRange objects ({ dataFilter, values, majorDimension? }).

data.description: Array of DataFilterValueRange objects. Example: [{ dataFilter: { a1Range: 'Sheet1!A1:B2' }, values: [['x','y'],['z','w']] }].

tools.name: batch_clear_values
tools.description: Atomically clear values from multiple A1 ranges in one request. Keeps formatting.

tools.name: batch_clear_values_by_filter
tools.description: Atomically clear values from multiple ranges selected by data filters.

tools.name: copy_sheet
tools.description: Copy a single sheet (tab) from one spreadsheet to another. Useful for templates: create a master sheet, then copy it into per-customer files. Returns the new sheet's properties (sheetId, title, etc.) in the destination spreadsheet.

sheetId.description: Numeric sheet ID of the tab to copy (NOT the sheet title). Get it from get_spreadsheet → sheets[].properties.sheetId.

destinationSpreadsheetId.description: Target spreadsheet ID where the sheet will be copied to.
